verses.text: Moses, eighty years old, is keeping his father-in-law's flock at the back of the desert. He reaches Horeb, the mountain of God — though he doesn't know it yet. Guidance: God often meets us in seasons that look like wasted time. The wilderness years are forming you, not benching you.

The angel of the LORD appears to Moses in a flame of fire out of the midst of a bush. The bush burns but is not consumed — a sign so strange it stops Moses in his tracks. Guidance: Pay attention to the small wonders God sends across your day. Most callings begin with a moment that interrupts your routine.

I will turn aside, says Moses, and see this great sight — why the bush is not burnt. He chooses to investigate. Guidance: God meets the curious. When something stirs in you that won't go away, don't shake it off. Turn aside and look longer.

When the LORD sees that he turned aside, God calls him out of the bush: Moses, Moses. Here am I. Draw not near; take off your shoes — the place you stand is holy ground. Guidance: Holiness asks for reverence, not performance. Sometimes the most honest worship is simply taking off your shoes and being still.

When the LORD sees that he turned aside, God calls him out of the bush: Moses, Moses. Here am I. Draw not near; take off your shoes — the place you stand is holy ground. Guidance: Holiness asks for reverence, not performance. Sometimes the most honest worship is simply taking off your shoes and being still.

I am the God of your father — the God of Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob. Moses hides his face; he is afraid to look at God. Guidance: When God identifies Himself by past faithfulness, He is telling you He has not changed. The God of your mother's prayers is still here today.

I have surely seen the affliction of My people in Egypt, says the LORD. I have heard their cry. I know their sorrows. I have come down to deliver them, to bring them up to a good land, a land flowing with milk and honey. Guidance: God's verbs are slow but never absent — seen, heard, known, come down. The cry you raised last year has not been lost.

I have surely seen the affliction of My people in Egypt, says the LORD. I have heard their cry. I know their sorrows. I have come down to deliver them, to bring them up to a good land, a land flowing with milk and honey. Guidance: God's verbs are slow but never absent — seen, heard, known, come down. The cry you raised last year has not been lost.

The cry of the children of Israel has come up to Me. I have seen their oppression. Come now, I will send you to Pharaoh, that you may bring out My people. Guidance: God's compassion always becomes a commission. Where He has comforted you, He will eventually send you back with grace for someone else.

The cry of the children of Israel has come up to Me. I have seen their oppression. Come now, I will send you to Pharaoh, that you may bring out My people. Guidance: God's compassion always becomes a commission. Where He has comforted you, He will eventually send you back with grace for someone else.

Who am I, that I should go to Pharaoh? Moses asks. God answers: I will certainly be with you — and this shall be the sign: when you have brought My people out, you shall serve God on this mountain. Guidance: God doesn't answer who am I with reassurances about you. He answers with I will be with you. That is the only credential you ever needed.

Who am I, that I should go to Pharaoh? Moses asks. God answers: I will certainly be with you — and this shall be the sign: when you have brought My people out, you shall serve God on this mountain. Guidance: God doesn't answer who am I with reassurances about you. He answers with I will be with you. That is the only credential you ever needed.

When I tell them the God of your fathers sent me, they will ask: what is His name? What shall I say? God answers: I AM THAT I AM. Say I AM has sent me to you. Guidance: God's name is a verb, not a snapshot. He simply is — eternally, faithfully, presently. When you panic about the future, return to this name.

When I tell them the God of your fathers sent me, they will ask: what is His name? What shall I say? God answers: I AM THAT I AM. Say I AM has sent me to you. Guidance: God's name is a verb, not a snapshot. He simply is — eternally, faithfully, presently. When you panic about the future, return to this name.

God adds: this is My name forever — the LORD God of Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob has sent you. This is My memorial for all generations. Guidance: The way God reveals Himself in your generation will be remembered by the next. Live like someone is watching how you trust.

Gather the elders, says God. Tell them: the LORD God of your fathers appeared to me. I have surely visited you. I will bring you up out of affliction into a land flowing with milk and honey. Guidance: Sometimes leadership is just being the one who tells others what God has been saying. You are not the answer; you are the messenger.

Gather the elders, says God. Tell them: the LORD God of your fathers appeared to me. I have surely visited you. I will bring you up out of affliction into a land flowing with milk and honey. Guidance: Sometimes leadership is just being the one who tells others what God has been saying. You are not the answer; you are the messenger.

They will listen, says God. Then you and the elders shall go to the king of Egypt and say: the LORD God of the Hebrews has met with us. Let us go three days into the wilderness to sacrifice. Guidance: Worship is at the center of the request. The first goal of freedom is not comfort — it is the chance to worship rightly.

I am sure the king of Egypt will not let you go — not by a mighty hand alone. I will stretch out My hand and strike Egypt with all My wonders. After that, he will let you go. Guidance: Resistance is not a sign you misheard God. It is sometimes the very setting in which His power becomes visible.

I am sure the king of Egypt will not let you go — not by a mighty hand alone. I will stretch out My hand and strike Egypt with all My wonders. After that, he will let you go. Guidance: Resistance is not a sign you misheard God. It is sometimes the very setting in which His power becomes visible.

And I will give this people favor in the sight of the Egyptians — when you go, you shall not go empty. Every woman shall ask her neighbor for silver, gold, clothing; you shall plunder Egypt. Guidance: God's deliverance often includes restitution for what was stolen. Years of bondage are not erased, but they are not unanswered either.

And I will give this people favor in the sight of the Egyptians — when you go, you shall not go empty. Every woman shall ask her neighbor for silver, gold, clothing; you shall plunder Egypt. Guidance: God's deliverance often includes restitution for what was stolen. Years of bondage are not erased, but they are not unanswered either.
